perturbation.original_text: Each Party will protect the confidentiality of the Confidential Information by using the same degree of care (but not less than a reasonable degree of care) it uses to protect the confidentiality of its own proprietary and co nfidential information.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party will protect the confidentiality of the Confidential Information by using a degree of care deemed appropriate by its internal policies, regardless of the care used to protect its own proprietary and confidential information.
perturbation.explanation: This changes the standard of care required to protect Confidential Information. The original text mandates 'the same degree of care' (but not less than a reasonable degree of care) used for its own information. Now, the degree of care is based on internal policies, potentially lower than what's used for its own confidential data, thus contradicting the original intent of equal protection. In the original, it guarantees a minimum degree of care. With this perturbation, the degree of care now depends on each party's internal policies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party will protect the confidentiality of the Confidential Information by using the same degree of care (but not less than a reasonable degree of care) it uses to protect the confidentiality of its own proprietary and co nfidential information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the standard of care for protecting Confidential Information. Originally, it mandated 'the same degree of care' as the party uses for its own information, with a reasonable degree of care as the minimum. The changed text allows the degree of care to be determined by internal policies, potentially lower than the care used for its own confidential data. This contradicts the contradicted text, which is the same as the original text, which requires the same minimum degree of care as the company uses for its own confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information of either Party may not be copied or reproduced by the other Party without the disclosing Party’s prior written consent.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information of either Party may be copied or reproduced by the other Party for internal use without the disclosing Party's prior written consent, provided the copies are destroyed after the agreement's term.
perturbation.explanation: This allows for copying and reproducing Confidential Information for 'internal use' without consent, directly contradicting the original restriction. While it mentions destruction later, the initial permission undermines the confidentiality. This is an in-text contradiction because previously it guaranteed not to copy any form of confidential information without prior consent. Now it can be copied for internal use.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information of either Party may not be copied or reproduced by the other Party without the disclosing Party’s prior written consent.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text prohibits any copying or reproduction of Confidential Information without prior written consent. The changed text now permits copying and reproduction for 'internal use' without such consent, provided the copies are destroyed after the agreement's term. This directly conflicts with the contradicted text, which states that 'Confidential Information of either Party may not be copied or reproduced by the other Party without the disclosing Party’s prior written consent.'

perturbation.original_text: Upon receipt of written notice requesting return of any Confidential Information, the recipient Party will promptly (a) deliver to the disclosing Pa rty all Confidential Information furnished by the disclosing Party to the recipient Party, together with copies thereof, and (b) destroy materials generated by the recipient Party that include or re late to any part of the Confidential Information (including notes, analyses and compilations) without retaining a copy of any such material. At the request of the disclosing Party, any such destruction shall be confirmed in writing by recipient Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon receipt of written notice requesting return of any Confidential Information, the recipient Party will make a good faith effort to return the Confidential Information, but is not required to destroy any electronic copies created for backup purposes. Furthermore, written confirmation of destruction is not required.
perturbation.explanation: This weakens the obligation to return and destroy Confidential Information. 'Good faith effort' is less stringent than 'will promptly deliver'. The exception for 'electronic copies' and removal of the confirmation requirement creates ambiguity and contradicts the clear instruction for full return and destruction. This introduces an in-text contradiction because the original text demands to return all confidential information and the recipient party is now allowed to keep a backup of it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon receipt of written notice requesting return of any Confidential Information, the recipient Party will promptly (a) deliver to the disclosing Pa rty all Confidential Information furnished by the disclosing Party to the recipient Party, together with copies thereof, and (b) destroy materials generated by the recipient Party that include or re late to any part of the Confidential Information (including notes, analyses and compilations) without retaining a copy of any such material. At the request of the disclosing Party, any such destruction shall be confirmed in writing by recipient Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the obligation to return and destroy Confidential Information. The original text and contradicted text state that the recipient party 'will promptly (a) deliver to the disclosing Pa rty all Confidential Information furnished by the disclosing Party to the recipient Party, together with copies thereof, and (b) destroy materials generated by the recipient Party that include or re late to any part of the Confidential Information (including notes, analyses and compilations) without retaining a copy of any such material.' However, the changed text states the recipient party will make a 'good faith effort' and can keep backup copies and does not require written confirmation of destruction. This directly contradicts the original text's and contradicted text's requirement for full return and destruction without retaining copies, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction as outlined in the perturbation explanation.

perturbation.original_text: The term of this Agreement shall continue dur ing the Parties’ business relationship and bind the Parties hereto for a period of three (3) years af ter the termination of all business relationships between the Parties. Notwithstanding the foregoing, th is Agreement shall survive with respect to all Confidential Information that is di sclosed before the termination.
perturbation.changed_text: The term of this Agreement shall continue dur ing the Parties’ business relationship and bind the Parties hereto for a period of one (1) year af ter the termination of all business relationships between the Parties, unless otherwise stipulated in writing between both parties. Notwithstanding the foregoing, th is Agreement shall only survive with respect to Confidential Information that is di sclosed before the termination if both parties have mutually confirmed and signed for it, by written consent.
perturbation.explanation: This significantly reduces the agreement's duration from three years post-termination to one year and adds a condition. It creates uncertainty about which duration applies. Also the statement 'Agreement shall only survive with respect to Confidential Information that is di sclosed before the termination if both parties have mutually confirmed and signed for it, by written consent' introduces another caveat that there must be written consent. This contradicts the original text, where the agreement originally was to survive for a set amount of time automatically, but with the perturbation, the agreement may not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term of this Agreement shall continue dur ing the Parties’ business relationship and bind the Parties hereto for a period of three (3) years af ter the termination of all business relationships between the Parties. Notwithstanding the foregoing, th is Agreement shall survive with respect to all Confidential Information that is di sclosed before the termination.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the duration of the agreement post-termination from three years to one year, introducing a direct conflict with the contradicted text, which specifies a three-year duration. Furthermore, it adds a condition for the survival of the agreement regarding confidential information (written consent), which contradicts the original text's automatic survival clause. This creates a strong in-text contradiction as the contradicted text clearly states an agreement duration of three years and automatically survival of confidential information.
